title: Graded same-lap LambdaRank with pit-imminence labels
summary: XGBoost’s ranking objectives support query-group training with pair construction focused on top positions.
rationale: XGBoost’s ranking objectives support query-group training with pair construction focused on top positions. Prior research already pointed toward same-lap ranking; the stronger variant is to rank drivers by pit imminence, not just a binary next-lap flag, using graded labels like pit in 1/2/3 laps.
implementation_hint: Create qid = Year/Race/LapNumber. On train only, derive graded labels such as 3 if pit next lap, 2 if pit in 2 laps, 1 if pit in 3 laps, 0 otherwise. Train `XGBRanker` with `objective='rank:ndcg'` and test both `lambdarank_pair_method='topk'` and `mean`. Blend its within-lap rank output with the current classifier OOF ranks.
expected_effect: Better separation among the few same-lap pit candidates; likely strongest in late-race and clustered-stop laps where absolute probabilities are less stable than ordering.
risk: Can overfit if query groups with very few positives dominate or if ranking output is used without a fold-safe blend. Public CV may rise while LB stays flat if query leakage remains.
